response:
Note: This is in an alternate universe. This shop, despite being awesome, never existed. It does in this alternate universe. We’ve loved being in this spot as part of the wonderful community. Unfortunately, this shop is now closed. The realities of retail in the shopping landscape have made this decision unavoidable. We want to take the opportunity to thank you for shopping with us for so many years. You can find all about the fictional brand and its related online MMO at a fictional website. Goodbye forever… This sign was posted to the doors of a fictional children’s shop on Wednesday. The image ends with a poignant “Goodbye forever…” above an image of a crying fictional character. The store closed on Wednesday after 12 years trading at a well-known street. The shop sold merchandise related to the animated series. The cartoon is a fictional cartoon about circular colorful animals and their fun adventures. The shop opened in 1995 and was originally a general toy shop akin to a fictional competitor, but in 2003 became focused on the then new surge in the popularity of the cartoon in the region. However, in June 2007, the store closed for the last time. The fictional group’s reasoning was the “realities of retail” was unavoidable. The shop was also closed so the fictional group could focus on business in its native country and develop more exciting new animated series or movies. 

A post from a local community member on 23 June 2007: It’s the end of an era, even bigger than the end of a fictional event, or even a fictional character leaving a fictional role. The famous shop has gone. After about 12 years of selling toys and 4 years of its golden age of merchandise, the store has unfortunately vanished. In its place is a rather upsetting image of the fictional character crying. It pains my heart to see the poor character like this. Apparently, “the realities of retail in the shopping landscape have made this change unavoidable.” What’s most striking is “Goodbye forever…” written in a melancholic font, and the most striking part is that it’s showing the fictional character crying. My kids will miss the shop dearly. There is another sign giving the channel numbers for a fictional TV channel that broadcasts the cartoon. Although there is an error, a fictional cable provider doesn't exist anymore, it's another fictional provider now.

Another community member posted on 23 June 2007: It’s a very sad state of affairs
